issues.title: Core tracker: CLI + HTML board + skill packaging
issues.description: The v1 build: dependency-free Python CLI, standalone HTML board view, agent skill packaging for Claude Code and Codex.

issues.title: jira.py CLI: init/add/move/comment/set/list/next/show/status/render
issues.description: 629-line stdlib-only CLI; JSON source of truth with auto re-render via save().

issues.title: Standalone HTML board: kanban, by-epic view, filters, detail modal
issues.description: Self-contained board.html with embedded data island; dark theme; no runtime deps beyond optional web fonts.

issues.title: Skill packaging for Claude Code (.claude) and Codex (.codex)
issues.description: SKILL.md workflows 1-4 + operating principles; Codex variant adds agents/openai.yaml.

issues.title: Docs and examples: README, references/schema.md, sample board + rendered HTML
issues.description: Install instructions (global/project), hand-driving guide, schema reference, consistent example board.

issues.title: Hardening: fixes from 2026-06-04 code review
issues.description: Seven confirmed bugs (each reproduced via CLI) plus test coverage and packaging-sync work.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Starting hardening pass: black-box test suite first (RED), then fixes for JT-7..JT-13 (GREEN), then .codex sync + example regeneration.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: High-effort /code-review of 34e4bca..HEAD (board redesign): 10 verified findings. 2 already tracked in JT-20 (onclick JS-escaping, unknown-status count mismatch); color-mix fallback concern from JT-20 was re-assessed and refuted (whole template requires color-mix, baseline since 2023). Remaining new findings proposed as follow-up tickets: epic-filter count/visibility inconsistencies, grandchild orphaning, lost sticky status summary, hardcoded 5-status CSS, missing artifact-sync test.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Incident 2026-06-06: board.html regressed to the pre-redesign UI. Root cause: the global skill copy at ~/.claude/skills/jira-tracker/scripts/jira.py (659 lines, pre-JT-19) was used for board writes, and every write re-renders board.html — stale generator overwrote the redesigned view. board.json data was unaffected. Fixed by re-rendering with the repo-local script (12/12 template markers restored, diff vs HEAD now data-only). Real-world confirmation of the stale-artifact risk behind JT-25.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Incident follow-up closed: user synced the global skill copy per README install steps (cp -R .claude/skills/jira-tracker ~/.claude/skills/). Verified: dirs byte-identical (852-line jira.py), and a render through the global path now produces the redesigned template (12/12 markers, board.html diff vs HEAD remains data-only).

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Hardening epic complete: all 2026-06-04 and 2026-06-06 review findings shipped — JT-20..34 (delegation/a11y/contrast/dynamic CSS/epic semantics/hash state/locking/escaping/guards/version stamp/docs/artifact sync) all Done. Final whole-diff review: zero critical/important findings, 130+ tests green, mirrors byte-identical.

issues.title: fuzzy() rejects documented status shorthands prog/review
issues.description: SKILL.md:42 and schema.md:50 promise 'prog'/'review' work, but fuzzy() (jira.py:90-100) is prefix-only and 'in progress' does not start with 'prog'. Fix: unique-substring fallback.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Fixed fuzzy() in scripts/jira.py: unique-fragment fallback after prefix matching, so prog/review resolve as documented; ambiguous input now lists its candidates. Tests: test_move_accepts_prog_shorthand / _review_shorthand.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: User-reviewed and committed in bbcc3ef.

issues.title: list --status <closed> returns nothing without --all
issues.description: Open-only filter at jira.py:236-237 runs after the explicit status filter and wipes Done/Cancelled results. Fix: explicit --status implies --all.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: cmd_list: an explicit --status filter now implies --all, so closed statuses are findable. Test: test_list_status_done_finds_closed_without_all.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: User-reviewed and committed in bbcc3ef.

issues.title: render mutates board.json (bumps project.updated)
issues.description: cmd_render (jira.py:306-309) goes through save(); a read-only view command rewrites the JSON and causes git diffs. Fix: render_html() directly.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: cmd_render now renders the view directly without save(); board.json is untouched. Test: test_render_does_not_modify_board_json.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: User-reviewed and committed in bbcc3ef.

issues.title: init accepts unvalidated --key; esc() misses single quotes
issues.description: init --key "A'B</script>" is accepted; keys are interpolated into onclick JS strings (jira.py:484) and esc() (:457) does not escape quotes. Fix: validate ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9]{1,9}$ and escape ' in esc().

comments.author: agent
comments.body: init validates --key against [A-Z][A-Z0-9]{1,9}; template esc() now escapes single quotes. Tests: test_init_rejects_invalid_key, test_template_esc_covers_single_quote. examples/board.html regenerated with the new template.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: User-reviewed and committed in bbcc3ef.

issues.title: Corrupt board.json shows raw traceback; writes not atomic
issues.description: load() (jira.py:55-60) has no JSONDecodeError handling; save() truncate-writes in place. Fix: friendly die() + temp-file os.replace writes.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: load() dies cleanly on JSONDecodeError; new write_atomic() (temp file + os.replace) used for both board.json and board.html. Tests: test_corrupt_json_dies_cleanly, test_save_leaves_no_temp_files.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: User-reviewed and committed in bbcc3ef.

issues.title: set --parent allows self-parent and cycles
issues.description: set KEY --parent KEY succeeds (jira.py:200-201). Fix: reject self-parent and walk ancestors to detect cycles.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: New valid_parent() helper rejects self-parenting and ancestry cycles in cmd_set. Tests: test_set_rejects_self_parent, test_set_rejects_parent_cycle.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: User-reviewed and committed in bbcc3ef.

issues.title: SKILL.md $jira alias pattern breaks under zsh
issues.description: jira="python3 path" then $jira cmd relies on word splitting; zsh (macOS default) does not split. Fix SKILL.md to README-style: jira=<path>; python3 $jira cmd.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: SKILL.md now stores only the script path in the variable and invokes via: python3 $jira (zsh-safe); schema.md wording corrected from unique-prefix to unique fragments. Test: test_skill_md_has_no_zsh_breaking_alias.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: User-reviewed and committed in bbcc3ef.

issues.title: Add black-box CLI test suite (stdlib unittest)
issues.description: tests/test_jira.py invoking the CLI via subprocess: regression tests for all commands + the seven review bugs. No third-party deps, matching project ethos.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Writing tests/test_jira.py: subprocess-driven CLI tests; failing tests for each review bug + regression coverage for existing behavior.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: tests/test_jira.py added: 26 black-box subprocess tests (14 regression, 10 review-bug, 2 packaging), stdlib unittest only, no deps. Full suite green. Run: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: User-reviewed and committed in bbcc3ef.

issues.title: Sync mechanism + drift check for .claude/.codex/global installs
issues.description: Installed ~/.claude copy found stale vs repo (missing 4430ba0). Add make sync/install + a test asserting the two in-repo trees are identical.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Half done: test_claude_and_codex_trees_in_sync now enforces byte-identical shared files across both trees. Remaining: make sync/install convenience targets. Global ~/.claude install refreshed manually on 2026-06-04.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Delivered across this session: TEMPLATE_VERSION stamp + newer-board refusal (JT-34) guards stale-CLI drift at runtime; mirror byte-equality tests + artifact-sync test (JT-25) catch .claude/.codex drift in CI; global ~/.claude copy synced and verified byte-identical (Task F). Remaining global-install drift is covered by the runtime version guard since tests cannot reach outside the repo.

issues.title: Add LICENSE file
issues.description: README positions the repo as a shareable template; no license present.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Added MIT LICENSE (copyright 2026 Nhan Thanh Phan) — user-selected over Apache-2.0/proprietary. Standard MIT text, no modifications.

issues.title: Board view enhancements: epic progress rollup, surface In Review items
issues.description: By-Epic view could show x/y done per epic; next/status could call out In Review items as awaiting human review. Also: no CLI command to edit project fields (e.g. repo URL) after init.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Partially delivered by JT-19: the By-Epic x/y-done progress rollup is live in the redesigned board. Remaining scope: next/status calling out In Review items, and a CLI command to edit project fields after init.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Partial overlap shipped in the v2 template work: per-epic progress rollup now exists (transitive done/total bar with overall-progress tooltip, JT-21/22). Remaining scope to evaluate: any further In-Review surfacing beyond the column + stats chips.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Implementing remaining scope: (1) next/status call out In Review issues as awaiting human review (text + --json), (2) new set-project command to edit project name/repo after init (key excluded — issue keys derive from it). TDD: tests first.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Remaining scope shipped: (1) next and status now call out In Review issues in an 'in review (awaiting human review)' section, text + --json (new in_review key in both shapes); the nothing-actionable message is preserved alongside it. (2) New set-project command edits --name/--repo after init ('' clears repo; blank name rejected; key not editable since issue keys derive from it; save re-stamps project.updated and re-renders). 10 new tests (TDD red-green), 148/148 green. Docs updated: SKILL.md command table, schema.md JSON shapes, README example — both mirrors synced. Epic progress rollup half was already delivered by JT-19/21/22.

issues.title: Always-on board reconciliation after every request in tracked repos
issues.description: In repos with .jira/board.json, every turn ends with an explicit board-reconciliation check once work/thinking completes: update if work state changed, stay silent otherwise. Write model decided 2026-06-04: tiered auto-apply - status moves + comments on actively-worked issues apply immediately and are reported; new issues, retitles, re-priorities still confirmed first.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Brainstorming/design in progress; the new fuzzy 'prog' shorthand works, as it happens.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Design approved (tiered auto-apply + approach B: skill policy + optional UserPromptSubmit hook guarantee layer). Spec committed at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-04-board-reconciliation-design.md, pending user review.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Shipped: SKILL.md Workflow 5 (end-of-turn reconcile, blocked-write surfacing, red-flags incl. over-track loophole fix), Tiered board writes principle (auto move/comment/render on in-progress or named issues; confirm add/set/seeding/reopens), README five-moments + tiered description + Guarantee layer section, scripts/install-board-hook.py (idempotent, type-guarded, symlink-safe, --global/--settings; 8 tests). Skill-TDD evidence: baseline A dropped a blocked Done-move silently -> GREEN A surfaces exact pending commands; baseline/GREEN B closed the 'too trivial to track' rationalization. Residual noted: one GREEN-B run attempted confirm-tier add directly (caught by permission layer). Suite 34/34; trees byte-identical; global install refreshed.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: User-reviewed and committed in 3324517; hook file commit authorized separately.

issues.title: Modern board UI: dual theme, epic filter + search, sticky toolbar, issue drawer
issues.description: Full HTML_TEMPLATE redesign (user-validated via brainstorm mockups): dark+light themes with FOUC-free bootstrap, localStorage persistence and system-preference default; auto-collapsing sticky toolbar (IntersectionObserver) holding epic/type/priority dropdowns with faceted counts, text search, conditional clear button; Linear-style right-side detail drawer with clickable parent link replacing the centered modal; By-Epic groups now derive from all epics (type filters no longer hide groups) with x/y-done progress bars. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-06-board-ui-redesign-design.md (local).

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Shipped on main: 2456dde (template + 4 new TestModernTemplate tests, 38/38 green), 36c4a57 (regenerated .jira/board.html + examples/board.html, byte-stable re-render), c6aa07a (README). Verified: per-task spec reviews, opus code-quality review (0 critical/important), final integration review (ready to ship), headless-Chrome runtime smoke (render() end-to-end, no console errors).

issues.title: Board template polish: data-key event delegation, parent-link a11y, color-mix fallbacks
issues.description: Minors from the 2026-06-06 board-redesign review: (1) replace inline onclick handlers with delegated listeners on data-key attributes — closes a latent JS-string escaping gap (unreachable via CLI-validated keys) and is less code; (2) drawer parent link is a no-href <a>, not keyboard-focusable. Pre-existing, not from this change: issues with a status outside BOARD.statuses count in the header total but render in no column.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Dropped item (3) color-mix fallbacks: the 2026-06-06 high-effort review re-verified it and refuted it as non-actionable — every badge/pill background in the template already requires color-mix (only the toolbar has a cascade fallback), and color-mix is baseline in all evergreen browsers since mid-2023, so no realistic environment degrades just the one border.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Task C starting: replacing all inline onclick with delegated data-key listeners (closes the JS-string escaping gap).

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Delegation half shipped in ea59540: zero inline onclick, data-key/data-action delegation, keyboard activation on cards/rows, parent link is a real anchor; keys never reach JS-string contexts. Remaining: drawer focus management + :focus-visible (Task D). Bonus fix ad14421: --file/--json now honored before the subcommand (argparse SUPPRESS).

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Fully shipped across C+D: zero inline onclick (delegated data-key/data-action), keyboard activation, real parent anchor, dialog focus management, :focus-visible. The JS-string escaping gap is structurally closed. Commits ea59540 + 4c2c4eb + 113dfdb.

issues.title: Epic-filter semantics inconsistent: dropdown counts, empty state, header visibility
issues.description: From the 2026-06-06 high-effort review of the redesign (jira.py ~633/691/706): (1) epic dropdown count uses the filtered set while the group progress denominator uses ALL unfiltered children — numbers diverge under any active filter (CONFIRMED); (2) a selected epic whose children are all filtered out still renders a header with 'no matching issues' because the suppression guard requires !fEpic (CONFIRMED); (3) epic-group visibility is driven solely by matching children, never testing the epic's own type/priority against active filters (PLAUSIBLE).

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Task C: unified epic-filter semantics (memberCount drives dropdown counts, group visibility, and noMatch state).

comments.author: agent
comments.body: memberCount (transitive members passing non-epic facets) now drives dropdown counts, group rendering, and the noMatch state; progress bar is explicitly overall/unfiltered with a title. Dropdown number == rendered content by construction. Commit ea59540.

issues.title: Grandchild sub-tasks fall into 'No Epic' — epic grouping matches direct parent only
issues.description: matchEpic/isOrphan/renderEpics (jira.py ~591/595/693) only test i.parent===epic.key. valid_parent imposes no type rule, so Sub-task→Story→Epic chains are permitted by the data model but render under 'No Epic' and are hidden when their epic is selected. Carried over from the old template; no such chain exists in shipped data yet.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Task C: transitive nearest-Epic-ancestor membership map (cycle-safe).

comments.author: agent
comments.body: EPIC_OF map: nearest-Epic-ancestor via cycle-safe parent-chain walk, built once per render; grandchildren attribute to the nearest epic, cycles degrade to orphans. Commit ea59540.

issues.title: Restore at-a-glance status counts while scrolled
issues.description: The redesign un-stickied the header: per-status count chips (#stats) and project name scroll away; the sticky toolbar carries only filters + mini key badge. Surface the counts in (or near) the sticky toolbar so the summary stays visible on long boards.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Task C: compact status chips inside the sticky toolbar when stuck.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Compact per-status dot+count strip (#ministats) in the toolbar, visible when stuck. Commit ea59540.

issues.title: Derive board grid tracks and status colors from BOARD.statuses
issues.description: CSS hardcodes repeat(5,...) (jira.py ~462) and 5 status color vars/maps (~380/580) while JS treats BOARD.statuses as dynamic (columns from STATUSES.map, ||'var(--muted)' fallbacks). A hand-edited statuses list (board.json is the documented source of truth) overflows the grid and renders grey columns. Generate track count and a color assignment from the status list at render time.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Task D: grid + status colors generated from BOARD.statuses (no repeat(5), no all-grey unknowns).

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Grid + STATUS_COLOR generated from board statuses: auto-fill capped at --ncols (no repeat(5)), canonical statuses keep theme vars, unknowns get deterministic theme-aware --fb-1..8 vars (AA-verified both themes, mod-8 recycle documented). Commits 4c2c4eb + 113dfdb.

issues.title: Add artifact regeneration-sync test for committed board.html files
issues.description: No test regenerates and diffs the committed examples/board.html / .jira/board.html against current jira.py output (tests only assert on temp-dir renders), yet README points users at examples/board.html. Verified in sync today; add a test rendering examples/sample-board.json and diffing against the committed artifact so template edits can't ship stale examples.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Task F starting: artifact regeneration-sync test, regenerate artifacts with final template, sync .codex + global mirrors.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: TestArtifactSync pins examples/board.html to the current template (byte-equality, render-is-read-only assertion); artifacts regenerated with template v2; .codex + global ~/.claude mirrors verified byte-identical. Commit cd889a2.

issues.title: Concurrent CLI writes lose updates; shared tmp file can corrupt board.json
issues.description: Reproduced 3/3: 10 parallel adds persist only 8-9 issues (load->mutate->save races, last writer wins); write_atomic uses a fixed board.json.tmp so concurrent writers interleave bytes and can leave invalid JSON. Fix: flock-based board.lock spanning load->save for all mutators + render, and unique per-process tmp names.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: board_lock (flock, blocking) spans load->save for all mutators + render; write_atomic uses mkstemp + chmod 0644 + os.replace with unlink-on-failure. Verified: 10 parallel adds -> 10 issues, counter 10. Commits e639062 + 5268403.

issues.title: U+2028/U+2029 unescaped in board.html embedded JSON payload
issues.description: render_html json.dumps(ensure_ascii=False) emits raw line/paragraph separators into the <script> block; </ is escaped but U+2028/29 are not. Escape them after the </ replace.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: U+2028/U+2029 escaped in the embedded payload after the </ escape; regression test with U+2028 in a title. Commit e639062.

issues.title: Drawer dialog semantics + focus management
issues.description: Drawer opens without moving focus, no Tab trap, no focus restore, no role=dialog/aria-modal/aria-labelledby. Make it a real dialog: focus close btn on open, trap Tab, Esc closes, restore opener focus on close, :focus-visible outlines.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Task D starting: role=dialog, aria-modal, labelledby, focus trap/restore.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Drawer is a real dialog: role/aria-modal/labelledby, focus to close btn on open, live-requery Tab trap, Esc, focus restore with data-key/#main fallbacks; global :focus-visible. Commits 4c2c4eb + 113dfdb.

issues.title: WCAG contrast pass for badges and faint tokens
issues.description: Measured failures: light-theme Story/Done green 2.82:1, pri-High orange 3.0:1, Bug red 3.89:1 on 14% tints; --faint (keys, epic progress) 2.65:1 light / 3.0:1 dark. Raise tint to ~24%, darken light greens and --faint, promote .ckey/.prog-n to --muted; verify >=4.5:1 computationally in both themes.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Task D: tint 14->24%, light-theme green/orange + faint token adjustments, computational AA verification.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Tint 24%, retuned light greens/orange/cancel + faint both themes, .ckey/.prog-n -> muted, priority pill tinted. Verified computationally: worst 4.76 light / 5.14 dark for tokens, 4.57 worst for the new --fb palette. Commits 4c2c4eb + 113dfdb.

issues.title: URL-hash deep links for filters, view, and open issue
issues.description: All view/filter/search/drawer state lives in JS vars and dies on reload; links are unshareable. Serialize state to location.hash (replaceState), parse on load, support bare #JT-7 shorthand, handle hashchange.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Task C: URL-hash state (replaceState) for view/filters/search/open issue incl. bare #JT-n shorthand.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: URL-hash state via URLSearchParams + replaceState (view/epic/type/pri/q/issue), bare #JT-n shorthand, hashchange listener with self-write guard; special chars round-trip. Commit ea59540.

issues.title: SKILL.md contract reconciliation (CLI table, lifecycle, multi-copy/concurrency)
issues.description: CLI table drifted (missing --assignee/--author/--force/--file); no lifecycle guidance (reopen/cancel/comments append-only/merge conflicts); no multi-copy or concurrency story (the 2026-06-06 incident class); auto-apply tier underspecified for stale In-Progress issues. Reconcile docs with final CLI surface.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Task E starting: SKILL.md CLI table regen against final --help, Workflow 6 lifecycle, multi-copy/concurrency section, tier examples; schema.md + README updates.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: SKILL.md: CLI table regenerated from --help (12 commands + global-flags table), Workflow 6 lifecycle & corrections, Multiple copies & concurrency section, tightened tiered-writes with worked micro-examples, session-scoped leave-alone, large-board + --json guidance. schema.md: template_version/blocked_by/board.lock/--json shapes/doctor codes/dynamic colors/hash deep-links. README updated. Every claim verified against code; spec+quality reviews fixed 6 wording inaccuracies. Commits 0facb67 + 3b463ba + 94ef220.

issues.title: Small CLI guards: reopen comment, empty title, list --parent validation, --file position
issues.description: move out of Done/Cancelled requires --comment; add/set reject whitespace titles; list --parent validates the key via find() instead of silent empty result; unify add/set parent validation in one resolve_parent helper; --file works before and after the subcommand.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Reopen-from-closed requires --comment; empty/whitespace titles rejected on add/set; list --parent validates via find(); resolve_parent unifies add/set; --file accepted before and after the subcommand. Commits e639062 + 5268403.

issues.title: Template polish: print styles, reduced-motion, linkify, theme sync, responsive step, search flex
issues.description: No @media print (sticky toolbar overlaps, dark wastes ink); no prefers-reduced-motion; URLs in descriptions/comments not clickable (escape-first linkify); theme not synced across tabs/OS changes; 1100-1200px responsive gap needs a 3-col step; search input fixed at 190px should flex.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Task D: print styles, reduced-motion, linkify, theme sync, responsive step, search flex.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: @media print (light tokens, hidden chrome, break-inside:avoid), prefers-reduced-motion, escape-first linkify with noopener, storage+matchMedia theme sync gated on override, auto-fill kills the 1100-1200px gap, search flexes 160-320px. Commit 4c2c4eb.

issues.title: Stale-generator guard: template version stamp + CLI warning
issues.description: 2026-06-06 incident: a stale global jira.py silently re-rendered board.html with an old template. Stamp TEMPLATE_VERSION into board.json on save and into board.html; load() dies with a clear message when the board stamp is newer than the CLI.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: TEMPLATE_VERSION=2 stamped by save(), embedded as HTML comment; load() dies cleanly when the board stamp is newer than the CLI (incl. non-int stamps). Commits e639062 + 5268403.

Template fixes go in the embedded template in scripts/jira.py (.claude AND .codex mirrors) followed by regenerating examples/board.html and .jira/board.html.

Factual claims verified against code: doctor's 12 codes, two web fonts, reopen guard, tiered writes.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: All six children shipped: 3 template bugs fixed (linkify, q trim, hash churn), artifact-sync test extended to .jira pair, template cleanups landed, docs wording aligned. 138/138 tests green; mirrors synced; both artifacts regenerated.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Follow-up fallout: rendering examples/sample-board.json created examples/board.lock which slipped into the epic commit — untracked it and generalized .gitignore from .jira/board.lock to board.lock anywhere (render creates the lock next to whatever board file it operates on). Commit 371e17a.

issues.title: linkify() captures escaped entities and trailing punctuation into hrefs
issues.description: linkify runs /https?:\/\/[^\s<]+/ on esc()-escaped text, so 'see "https://x.com"' becomes href='https://x.com"' — entities ("/>/') and trailing ./) are glued into the URL, producing broken links. Fix in the template source in scripts/jira.py (both mirrors) + regenerate.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Fixing linkify in the embedded template: stop URL match at escaped-entity boundaries ("/>/'/<) while keeping & in query strings, and trim trailing punctuation.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Rewrote linkify: regex now keeps & inside URLs but stops at "/>/'/< entity boundaries, and peels trailing punctuation plus unbalanced ')' out of the anchor. Verified with 8 node test cases incl. Wikipedia-style balanced parens and query strings.

issues.title: Deep-link #q= param not trimmed — URL vs typed search filter differently
issues.description: applyHash sets q=p.get('q')||'' untrimmed while the input handler does e.target.value.trim(); #q=%20refund%20 fails to match what typing the same text matches. Trim in applyHash.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Trim q when restoring from the hash in applyHash, matching the input handler's .trim().

comments.author: agent
comments.body: applyHash now trims the q param ((p.get('q')||'').trim()), matching the live input handler — deep links with stray whitespace filter identically to typed queries.

issues.title: syncHash fires replaceState on every render; hashchange double-renders with two hash writes
issues.description: syncHash has no s===lastHash short-circuit, so every undebounced keystroke calls history.replaceState (Safari rate-limits ~100/30s and then throws). The hashchange handler runs render()+openIssue()/closeDrawer(), each calling syncHash — full board+drawer rebuild and two writes per back/forward nav.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Short-circuit syncHash when serialized state and URL hash are both unchanged; seed lastHash in the hashchange handler so the post-navigation render doesn't rewrite the hash it just consumed.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: syncHash short-circuits when serialized state and the URL hash are both unchanged (no more per-keystroke replaceState; Safari rate-limit safe). hashchange handler seeds lastHash before applying, so the post-navigation render no longer rewrites the hash it just consumed — one canonicalizing write max per navigation.

issues.title: Extend artifact-sync test to the .jira/board.json → .jira/board.html pair
issues.description: TestArtifactSync only drift-guards examples/; .jira/board.html is co-committed with board.json but nothing verifies they match (the prompt hook only echoes a reminder, no pre-commit/CI, and the version guard only fires on newer-than-CLI boards). Parametrize the byte-identity check over both pairs.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Parametrizing the byte-identity drift check over both committed json→html pairs (examples/ and .jira/).

comments.author: agent
comments.body: TestArtifactSync now parametrizes (via subTest) over both committed pairs: examples/sample-board.json→examples/board.html and .jira/board.json→.jira/board.html. Red-green verified: a one-byte-stale .jira/board.html fails the suite with a regeneration hint.

issues.title: Template v2 cleanups: shared pill/badge helpers, unified epic-membership pass, one-time epic index, drop openerKey, prune dead CSS
issues.description: Verified cleanups: (1) status-pill/type-badge markup hand-written at 7 sites (tint 14→24 was edited in lockstep this diff) — extract helpers; (2) epic-membership predicate open-coded at 3 sites, already diverging at lines 463/464 — one per-render bucketing pass; (3) buildEpicIndex rebuilt every render though BOARD is immutable — build once after parse; (4) openerKey duplicates openKey — capture a local in closeDrawer; (5) print @media enumerates toolbar children already hidden by .toolbar{display:none} (keep #sentinel) and .epic-head:focus-visible can never match (no tabindex).

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Template cleanups: pill/badge helpers, single membership pass, one-time epic index, drop openerKey, prune dead print/focus CSS.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Done in template v2: (1) badge()/typeBadge()/statusPill() helpers replace 8 hand-written pill/badge sites; (2) buildMembership() does one bucketing pass per render (MEMBERS/MEMBERS_ALL/ORPHANS) replacing per-epic full scans in memberCount/renderEpics — node equivalence check passed across 6 facet combos on the live board; (3) buildEpicIndex now runs once at load (BOARD is immutable in the browser); (4) openerKey removed, closeDrawer captures a local; (5) pruned dead CSS: print block child enumeration (toolbar already hidden, #sentinel kept) and unreachable .card/.row/.epic-head:focus-visible selectors ([tabindex] covers the focusable ones).

issues.title: Align SKILL.md version-guard wording with the actual mechanism (README done)
issues.description: SKILL.md ('Prefer the repo-local copy' section, both .claude and .codex mirrors) still says the version stamp prevents 'silent data corruption'; the guard only refuses an older CLI on a newer board, mainly preventing a stale embedded template from regressing the rendered board. README.md was already fixed on 2026-06-07.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: README.md half done (2026-06-07): reworded lines 31-34 to describe the real mechanism — older CLI refuses a newer board, preventing a stale embedded template from regressing the rendered board. Remaining: the same 'silent data corruption' phrase in SKILL.md, .claude and .codex mirrors.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Rewording the version-guard backstop sentence in SKILL.md, both mirrors.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: SKILL.md backstop sentence reworded in both mirrors: an older copy refuses a board written by a newer one, preventing a stale embedded template from regressing the rendered board. README half was done earlier today.

issues.title: Build sync mechanism descoped from JT-15: derive .codex from .claude + refresh global installs
issues.description: JT-15 shipped only the drift-detection test; the sync/install mechanism was descoped. Today the .claude→.codex mirror was hand-copied 3x and the global ~/.claude copy went stale again. Build scripts/sync.py: copy .claude/skills/jira-tracker → .codex/skills/jira-tracker (the single source is .claude), and with --global also refresh ~/.claude and ${CODEX_HOME:-~/.codex}. The byte-equality test stays as the backstop; README install snippets collapse to one command.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: TDD: writing tests for scripts/sync.py (mirror shared files .claude→.codex; --global refreshes ~/.claude and $CODEX_HOME/~/.codex), then implementing.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Built scripts/sync.py: mirrors the 5 shared files .claude→.codex (single source = .claude; codex-only agents/openai.yaml preserved), --check verifies (exit 1 on drift, CI backstop), --global refreshes ~/.claude and $CODEX_HOME:-~/.codex via clean copytree (excludes __pycache__/*.pyc/board.lock). 6 new tests (TDD), 153/153 green. sync.py added to the byte-equality shared list and committed into both trees. Ran --global for real: the stale ~/.claude copy flagged in this session's scan is now byte-identical to the repo. README gained a maintainer section; replaces the 3x hand-cp workflow used earlier today.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Post-review hardening of scripts/sync.py (/code-review, 8 findings): (1) source-of-truth now anchored to the .claude tree via _find_repo() instead of __file__ position, so running the byte-identical .codex copy no longer inverts the copy direction; (2) --check and --global made mutually exclusive (argparse) — previously --check short-circuited and silently skipped --global; (3) empty-string HOME/CODEX_HOME now falls back correctly (or, not get-default) so --global can't rmtree under cwd; (4) _require_src() gives a clear error when a SHARED entry is missing from source instead of a bare FileNotFoundError. Tests: SHARED list deduped to a single source (imported from sync.py; JT-15 byte-equality test + TestSyncScript share it); TestSyncScript now runs against an isolated temp copy of both trees so the suite never mutates the real .codex files. 154/154 green; .codex re-synced byte-identical.

issues.title: CLI capability upgrades: CI, history, bulk ops, search, report
issues.description: Batch of upgrades suggested after the JT-48 review: wire up CI to enforce the sync/test backstops, and extend the tracker CLI with activity history, bulk move/set, full-text search, and a metrics report.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: All 5 children Done. Suite 154->191 tests green; .codex re-synced byte-identical; smoke-tested in a temp board. Suggested after the JT-48 code review.

issues.title: GitHub Actions CI: run test suite + sync --check on push/PR to main
issues.description: Adds .github/workflows/ci.yml (matrix py 3.10-3.12). Makes the README's 'drift never reaches main' guarantee real — previously no CI existed.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Implemented via subagent. .github/workflows/ci.yml: push+PR to main, ubuntu-latest, py 3.10/3.11/3.12 matrix, runs 'unittest discover -s tests' and 'sync.py --check'. Stdlib-only, no pip.

issues.title: Activity history: append-only status-transition log per issue
issues.description: Each issue gets a history array of {at,from,to}. Seeded on add, appended on move (no dup on no-op). Shown in 'show'. Backward-compatible with older boards lacking the field.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Implemented (TDD). history=[{at,from,to}] seeded on add, appended on move only when status changes; shown in 'show'; missing-history boards handled everywhere; schema.md documents it.

issues.title: Bulk move/set: operate on multiple issue keys atomically
issues.description: move JT-1 JT-2 Done and set JT-1 JT-2 --priority High. Validate-all-first: an invalid key/value aborts the whole batch with zero writes; single save/render.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Implemented (TDD). move/set take nargs='+' keys; validate-all-first atomicity (invalid key/value -> zero writes); single save/render; per-issue history recorded; single-key usage unchanged.

issues.title: search command: case-insensitive full-text find across all issues
issues.description: Searches key/title/description/labels/components/comments across all statuses; reports match location; supports --json. Reuses cmd_list formatting.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Implemented (TDD). cmd_search across key/title/desc/labels/components/comments, all statuses, case-insensitive, match-location annotation, --json. Factored _format_issue_line out of cmd_list (no dup).

issues.title: report command: board metrics summary
issues.description: Counts by status/type/priority, stale count, and cycle time (creation -> first terminal transition, using history). Robust to issues without history; supports --json.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Implemented (TDD). cmd_report: totals + by status/type/priority, stale (reuses _is_stale), cycle time = created->first transition into CLOSED_STATUSES (avg+median), robust to no-history, --json.

issues.title: Skill: offer board creation via AskUserQuestion multiple-choice prompt
issues.description: Users often missed the plain-text 'create a Jira-style board?' offer. SKILL.md Step 1 now instructs asking via the AskUserQuestion tool (interactive multiple-choice, blocks until answered) with Yes / Not-now options, falling back to plain text in non-Claude-Code harnesses. Propagated to .codex mirror and global installs via sync.py --global.

issues.title: Step 1 board-creation prompt: resolve 'Not now' vs 'always offer' contradiction and harness/headless gaps
issues.description: Code review of JT-55 found 6 issues: always-offer rule overrides the Not-now suppression; .codex mirror's primary instruction names a Claude-Code-only tool; session-scoped suppression isn't durable (add a .jira/.no-board marker); no headless/subagent guidance; fallback drops the Not-now option; 'Yes, create the board' label implies immediate creation.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Rewriting SKILL.md Step 1: harness-neutral prompt instruction, durable .jira/.no-board opt-out, headless guidance, fallback carries options, no-re-ask carve-out on the summary rule, clarified Yes-option description. Will sync mirrors + global installs and run tests.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Rewrote SKILL.md Step 1 (lines 105-142): primary instruction is now harness-neutral ('the harness's interactive multiple-choice prompt tool — AskUserQuestion in Claude Code'); added a third option 'No, don't ask again' backed by a durable .jira/.no-board marker checked at the top of Step 1; added headless/subagent guidance (proceed as Not-now, mention the offer in the summary); plain-text fallback now carries the options and maps 'no' to Not-now; summary/review offer is at-most-once-per-session and respects declines/marker; Yes-option description notes the seed plan is reviewed before writing. Propagated via sync.py --global (.codex mirror + both global installs); 191 tests pass.

issues.title: Skill: ask git-tracking preference for .jira/ at board creation
issues.description: Many users don't want .jira/ pushed to git. Step 2 now asks (interactive prompt) how to handle it: commit the board (default), append .jira/ to .gitignore (shared ignore), or append to .git/info/exclude (local-only ignore). Headless runs default to committing. Operating principles updated to reflect the choice.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: SKILL.md Workflow 1 Step 2 now asks (interactive prompt, may ride alongside seed-plan approval): commit .jira/ (default, also the headless default), append .jira/ to .gitignore (shared ignore, board stays machine-local), or append to .git/info/exclude (local-only ignore); skipped for non-git repos, ignore entry applied after init. Operating principles bullet updated to honor the choice; README mentions the new question. Synced .codex mirror + global installs via sync.py --global; 191 tests pass.
